desc: Introduced. The common garden snail of LA — originally brought by French immigrants for escargot. Now the most abundant land snail.

fm.Shell: Brown with dark spiral bands; globose
fm.Behavior: Nocturnal; aggregates in moist shelters; mucus trails; hermaphrodite

hp: Garden snail/slug. Nocturnal herbivore. Preys on snail. Family Helicidae.

desc: White garden snail — introduced Mediterranean snail notorious for climbing vertical surfaces — fence posts, walls, and vegetation — en masse during summer dormancy (aestivation). Variable banding on white shell. Major agricultural pest. Thousands can aggregate on single structure.

fm.Shell: White/cream with variable brown spiral bands
fm.Behavior: Climbs posts, fences, walls in hot weather to escape ground heat; mass aggregations
fm.Season: Year-round; climbs structures in summer heat

hp: Garden and crop pest. Herbivore on cultivated plants. Preys on snail. Family Helicidae.

desc: Introduced predatory snail released as biocontrol for brown garden snails. Elongated shell with broken-off tip (decollate).

fm.Shell: Conical (not globose); brown; typically with broken-off apex
fm.Behavior: Predatory — eats brown garden snail eggs and juveniles; nocturnal

hp: Decollate snail — introduced predatory snail deliberately released to biocontrol brown garden snails (Cornu aspersum) in CA citrus groves.

desc: Native LA endemic — one of several shoulderband species restricted to SoCal coastal sage scrub. Consconcern as habitat shrinks.

fm.Shell: Brown with dark shoulder band; globose
fm.Behavior: Aestivates sealed to rocks/branches in dry season; active after rain
fm.Habitat: Coastal sage scrub, chaparral — native scrub remnants

hp: In coastal sage scrub habitat. Associated with Black Sage.

desc: Trask's shoulderband snail — native California land snail with brown shell and pale shoulderband. Found in moist canyons. Declining due to development and drought.
hp: Native land snail of coastal/foothill habitats. Conservation concern. Preys on snail. Family Helminthoglyptidae. Catalina.

desc: Three-banded garden slug — introduced slug with pale body and three dark longitudinal bands (sometimes faint). Very common garden pest. Nocturnal herbivore on seedlings and tender plants. Mucus trail prominent. Originally from Iberian Peninsula. Year-round in irrigated LA gardens.

fm.Color: Pale gray-brown with 2 dark lateral bands; pale keel
fm.Behavior: Nocturnal; damages seedlings; hides under boards/pots by day

hp: Garden and crop pest. Herbivore on cultivated plants. Preys on slug. Family Limacidae.

desc: Introduced. Large, spotted slug. Famous for spectacular aerial mating — pair suspends from mucus strand, intertwining.

fm.Color: Gray-brown with dark spots/leopard pattern; pale sole
fm.Behavior: Spectacular aerial mating (hanging from mucus rope); nocturnal
fm.Diet: Fungi, decaying matter; also other slugs (partially predatory)

hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on slug.

desc: Gray field slug — introduced from Europe. Small (40-50mm), gray with dark reticulate pattern. The single most destructive slug pest of agriculture worldwide — damages lettuce, strawberries, cereals, and ornamentals. Mucus milky white (diagnostic). Nocturnal. Enormous reproductive capacity.

fm.Color: Gray to cream; dark reticulated pattern; milky white mucus when irritated
fm.Behavior: Prodigious crop pest; leaves silver mucus trails
fm.Habitat: Agricultural, gardens — any moist cultivated area

hp: World's most destructive slug pest of agriculture (South 1992, Slug Pests). Damages lettuce, strawberries, cereals. Nocturnal. Mucus milky-white.

desc: Native. Bright yellow — North America's second-largest terrestrial gastropod. At southern range limit in San Gabriel Mtns. Indicator of old-growth moisture.

fm.Behavior: Slow; produces copious mucus; recycles nutrients; indicator of moist old forest
fm.Habitat: Moist redwood/mixed forest — Big Santa Anita Canyon, Mt. Wilson area
fm.Season: Active rainy season; rare in LA County

hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Largest.

desc: Green valve snail — tiny freshwater snail of clean springs and well-oxygenated streams. An indicator species for high water quality — disappears when water is polluted or diverted. Declining across western NA with spring habitat loss. Operculate (has a trap-door closing shell).

fm.Behavior: Grazes algae in clean springs and streams
fm.Habitat: Clean springs, slow streams — indicator of water quality

hp: Stream and river habitats. Aquatic or semi-aquatic lifestyle. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Introduced Mediterranean snail. Large, pale shell with brown bands. Found in hot dry areas — more heat-tolerant than garden snail.

fm.Shell: Pale cream/white with brown spiral bands; flattened
fm.Behavior: Aestivates sealed to walls/rocks; more heat-tolerant than Cornu

hp: Garden snail/slug. Nocturnal herbivore. Preys on snail.

desc: Grove snail — highly variable polymorphic snail from Europe. Shell can be yellow, pink, or brown with 0–5 dark bands. Classic genetics study organism. Established in LA.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Family Helicidae.

desc: Pellucid glass snail — tiny snail with paper-thin, nearly transparent shell. Body too large to fully retract — intermediate between snail and slug (semi-slug). In moist leaf litter and moss. Active in cool weather when other snails dormant. One of few snails active in winter.

fm.Shell: Glassy, transparent; very thin; body partly exposed
fm.Behavior: Cannot fully retract into shell; inhabits leaf litter
fm.Habitat: Moist woodland leaf litter, under logs

hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny. Glass.

desc: Asian trampsnail — small brown snail (12-16mm) with prominent dark spiral band on last whorl. Introduced from East Asia, now a pan-tropical invasive. Garden pest eating seedlings and leafy crops. Nocturnal. Distinguished from Cernuella by darker color and single bold band. Year-round in irrigated LA gardens.
hp: Garden and crop pest. Herbivore on cultivated plants. Preys on snail. Family Camaenidae.

desc: Jet slug — dark keeled slug (diagnostic ridge along back). Underground feeder — damages roots, tubers, and bulbs below soil surface. Serious garden pest, harder to control than surface slugs. Introduced from western Europe. Jet-black coloring distinctive.
hp: Garden and crop pest. Herbivore on cultivated plants. Preys on slug. Soil-dwelling.

desc: Walker's shoulderband — native land snail of coastal sand dunes and stabilized dunes. Tan with variable banding. NatureServe G2 (imperiled) due to severe dune habitat loss across SoCal coastline. One of several declining native Helminthoglypta — LA's coastal development has eliminated most historic dune populations.

fm.Shell: ½ in–⅔ in; brown with fine ribbing

hp: Coastal sand dune specialist — declining habitat.

desc: Golden apple snail — large freshwater snail (shell to 65mm). Among the world's worst invasive species. Major pest of rice paddies across Asia. Distinctive bright pink egg masses above waterline. Voracious herbivore. Periodically detected in LA waterways — CDFA quarantine species.
hp: Pome fruit associate — on apple or pear (Malus/Pyrus). Large (65mm).

desc: New Zealand mudsnail — tiny (4-6mm) freshwater snail and one of the most destructive aquatic invasives worldwide. Reproduces by parthenogenesis — single individual can found a colony. Reaches densities of 500,000/m². Displaces native invertebrates. Found in LA-area streams.

fm.Shell: ⅛ in–¼ in; dark brown aquatic snail

hp: Freshwater aquatic habitat — streams and ponds. Preys on snail.

desc: Giant African snail — massive snail reaching 8 inches (200mm). USDA quarantine pest — periodically detected and eradicated in SoCal. Vectors rat lungworm parasite. Voracious — eats 500+ plant species plus stucco (for calcium). Illegal to keep as pet in USA.
hp: Giant African snail — USDA quarantine pest. Vectors rat lungworm (Angiostrongylus). Eats 500+ plant species plus stucco. Illegal in US.

desc: Worm amber snail — tiny translucent amber-colored snail in damp vegetation at stream and pond margins. Thin fragile shell barely covers body (semi-slug). Indicator of healthy riparian habitat. Part of the Succineidae — a family adapted to wetland-terrestrial interface.

fm.Shell: ⅛ in–⅓ in; translucent amber; thin fragile

hp: Stream and river habitats. Aquatic or semi-aquatic lifestyle. Preys on snail. Riparian.

desc: Garlic glass snail — small translucent snail that produces distinctive garlic odor when handled or disturbed. Predatory — hunts other small snails, slugs, and invertebrates. European introduction. Under rocks and logs in moist habitats. The garlic smell is diagnostic.

fm.Behavior: Smells of garlic when handled; predatory

hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Family Oxychilidae.

desc: Cellar glass snail — small translucent snail common in cellars, greenhouses, under flowerpots, and in damp walls. Predatory — feeds on other small snails and slugs. European introduction. Named 'cellarius' for its association with dark damp cellars. Smooth glassy shell.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Family Oxychilidae.

desc: Garden arion — small dark slug (30-40mm). Major garden pest throughout temperate regions — attacks seedlings, strawberries, and leafy vegetables at ground level. Nocturnal. European introduction. Mucus dark orange-yellow. Sole orange (diagnostic). Extremely common in irrigated LA gardens.
hp: Garden and crop pest. Herbivore on cultivated plants. On strawberr.

desc: Kerry slug — spotted slug primarily known from Ireland; at extreme range edge in western NA. Feeds on lichens on rocks. Conservation interest species.
hp: Feeds on lichens and bark microflora. Preys on slug.

desc: Critically imperiled (G1) snail endemic to northern LA County. Burrowing species found only in San Gabriel Mountains foothills. Observed in City Nature Challenge 2024.

fm.Habitat: San Gabriel Mountain foothills — underground burrows

hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Montane.

desc: Small predatory snail that hunts other snails. Sharp radular tooth. Native to Pacific Coast forests. Found in moist canyon habitats in Santa Monica and San Gabriel Mountains.

fm.Habitat: Moist canyons — under logs, leaf litter
fm.Behavior: Predatory — eats other snails and slugs

hp: Snail/slug predator. Mountain habitat.

desc: Small translucent amber-yellow snail of moist habitats. Very thin fragile shell. Found on vegetation near streams and in irrigated gardens. Native California species.

fm.Color: Translucent amber-yellow; very thin fragile shell

hp: Garden snail/slug. Nocturnal herbivore. Preys on snail. Family Succineidae.

desc: Black turban snail — dark conical marine snail, the most abundant intertidal gastropod on LA County's rocky shores. Grazes microalgae. Shells commonly colonized by hermit crabs after death. Long-lived (to 30 years). Keystone grazer maintaining algal community structure on rocks.
hp: Rocky intertidal algae grazer on LA County shores. Important in structuring algal community. Preys on snail. Urban.

desc: Owl limpet — large (up to 90mm) territorial marine limpet that 'farms' algae. Defends territory on rock surfaces, pushing away competitors — creating an algae 'garden' for its exclusive grazing. Keystone intertidal species. Harvested historically by indigenous peoples. Declining.

fm.Color: Brown with pale mottling; flat conical

hp: Intertidal algae grazer — maintains algal community structure on rocky shores.

desc: Dark elongated marine snail. Extremely abundant in salt marsh mudflats. Important shorebird food. Wetland indicator.
hp: Marine invertebrate of socal waters. Important prey for insectivorous birds.

desc: Checkered periwinkle — small snail abundant on upper intertidal rocks. Extremely common — often the most numerous visible snail on rocky shores. Grazes microalgae. Tolerates long air exposure at high tide line. Checkered pattern on shell. Important prey for shorebirds.
hp: Rocky intertidal algae grazer — most abundant upper-shore snail on LA County rocks. Important prey for shorebirds. Tolerates long air exposure.

desc: Marine snail with thin globular shell. Lives in eelgrass beds and mudflats. Herbivorous. Beautiful amber-spotted shell.

fm.Color: Amber with brown spots; globular thin shell

hp: On grasses and forbs in grassland. Preys on snail.

desc: Large sea slug (not a snail) up to 16 in. Purple ink defense. Major neuroscience model organism — Eric Kandel won Nobel Prize studying its neurons.

fm.Note: Nobel Prize model organism for neuroscience

hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Large (16 in).

desc: Spectacular large nudibranch. Brilliant red with white edges. Swims by undulating body margin like a flamenco dancer.
hp: Nudibranch — specialist predator of sessile marine invertebrates.

desc: Only cone snail on US Pacific coast. Venomous (mild). Predatory — harpoons and paralyzes prey with venomous radular tooth. Rocky intertidal.

fm.Warning: Venomous — mild sting; predatory on worms/snails

hp: Snail/slug predator. Coastal habitat.

desc: Large reddish-brown to chocolate slug. Common garden pest. Originally from Europe. Up to 6 inches. Eats seedlings and soft fruit.
hp: Garden and crop pest. Herbivore on cultivated plants. Preys on slug.

desc: Yellow cellar slug — large slug up to 5 inches with yellow-green coloring and darker mottling. Nocturnal. Found in basements, cellars, greenhouses. Feeds on fungi and decaying vegetation.
hp: Mycetophagous — feeds on fungi. Fungal associate — see lafungi.org. Preys on slug.

desc: Draparnaud's glass snail — small translucent amber-brown snail with glossy shell. Predatory — eats other snails and slugs. Introduced from Europe. Very common under pots and debris.

fm.Behavior: Predatory on other snails and slugs

hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Family Zonitidae.

desc: Southern flatcoil — tiny flat-coiled snail. Shell wider than tall with visible ribbing. Found in leaf litter and under debris in moist gardens. Introduced from southeastern US.

fm.Color: Brown; flat disc-shaped shell with fine ribbing

hp: On Asteraceae (composites) — daisies, sunflowers, sagebrush. Preys on snail.

desc: Rounded snail — small disc-shaped snail with brown mottled shell. Very common in gardens under rocks and logs. Originally from Europe. Important leaf-litter decomposer.
hp: Wood decomposer — breaks down dead wood. Preys on snail.

desc: Salt-and-pepper nudibranch — tiny white dorid with black spots. Found in rocky intertidal zone on sponges. Well-camouflaged. One of the smaller nudibranchs.
hp: Intertidal nudibranch — specialist predator of sessile marine invertebrates. Tiny. Salt-and-pepper.

desc: Meadow slug — small brown slug of moist habitats. Can self-fertilize (rare among slugs). Very common in irrigated gardens. Feeds on seedlings and tender leaves.
hp: Garden snail/slug. Nocturnal herbivore. Preys on slug.

desc: Plain cactus snail — native California land snail found on and under prickly pear cactus. Pale shell with dark band. Aestivates sealed to cactus pads during summer drought.

fm.Color: Pale with dark band; attached to cactus pads

hp: Specialist on cactus (cactus spp.). On Opuntia (cactus). Family Helminthoglyptidae.

desc: Wreathed cactus snail — native California land snail with banded shell. Found on and under prickly pear cactus in coastal sage scrub. Aestivates attached to cactus pads.

fm.Color: Pale with brown bands; on cactus

hp: Sage; cactus. Associated with Black Sage and Coastal Prickly Pear. On Opuntia (cactus). Family Helminthoglyptidae.

desc: Poulson's rock snail — native California land snail found in rocky outcrops and talus slopes. Restricted range in LA County mountains. Conservation concern.
hp: Native land snail of coastal/foothill habitats. Conservation concern. Preys on snail. Montane.

desc: Kellett's cactus snail — native land snail of coastal sage scrub. Found under prickly pear cactus. Shell with distinctive banding. California endemic.

fm.Color: Pale with dark band; under cactus

hp: Sage; cactus. Associated with Black Sage and Coastal Prickly Pear. On Opuntia (cactus). Family Helminthoglyptidae.

desc: Small orb snail — tiny native land snail of moist canyon habitats. Brown disc-shaped shell. Found in leaf litter under oaks. Easily overlooked.
hp: On coast live oak (quercus agrifolia). Associated with Coast Live Oak. On Quercus (oak). Tiny.

desc: Pinhead snail — extremely tiny introduced snail. Shell under 3mm. Found in gardens, greenhouses, and disturbed habitats worldwide. Nearly invisible without hand lens.
hp: Found in and around buildings and structures. Preys on snail.

desc: Glossy pillar snail — small elongated translucent snail of moist habitats. Shiny smooth shell. Found in irrigated gardens and near streams. Introduced from Europe.
hp: Garden snail/slug. Nocturnal herbivore. Preys on snail. Elongated.

desc: Conspurcate snail — small pale snail with hairy shell surface. Introduced from Mediterranean. Common in disturbed urban habitats. Aggregates on walls and fences.

fm.Color: Pale with dark mottling; hairy shell surface

hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Urban.

desc: Gabb's shoulderband snail — native California land snail of coastal scrub. Dark shell with pale band. Found under debris in moist microhabitats. Declining.
hp: Native CA land snail — calcium-dependent. Chaparral/oak woodland leaf litter. Preys on snail.

desc: Obese land snail — small round native snail of moist canyon habitats. Restricted range in coastal mountains. Conservation concern.
hp: Native California land snail — endemic radiation in LA County foothills. Preys on snail. Montane.

desc: Cut rock snail — small native land snail of rocky outcrops. Flattened shell adapted to crevice-dwelling. Found on sandstone formations in mountains.

fm.Color: Brown; flattened shell for rock crevices

hp: Native land snail of coastal/foothill habitats. Preys on snail. Montane.

desc: Forty-ribbed cardita — small ribbed marine bivalve of rocky intertidal and subtidal zones. Found attached to rocks. Common in LA County tidepools.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Family Carditidae.

desc: Small-penised slug — small keeled slug of gardens and moist habitats. Nocturnal. Introduced from Europe. Feeds on seedlings and fungi. Common under pots.
hp: Mycetophagous — feeds on fungi. Fungal associate — see lafungi.org. Preys on slug. Family Limacidae.

desc: Solander's trivia — small orange-pink marine snail (coffee bean shell) found under rocks in low intertidal. Round polished shell resembling a tiny cowry.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Tiny. Solander's.

desc: Manila clam — edible marine bivalve introduced from Asia for aquaculture. Found in bays and estuaries. Important commercially. Naturalized.

fm.Color: Variable brown/gray; concentric and radial ribs

hp: Marine filter feeder — processes seawater for food particles. Preys on ant.

desc: Onyx slipper shell — small dark marine snail that stacks in chains on rocks. Filter-feeder. Changes sex — male when young, female when old. Rocky intertidal.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Family Calyptraeidae.

desc: Valentian slug — medium keeled slug of gardens. Pale with darker lateral bands. Introduced from Iberian Peninsula. Nocturnal. Common garden pest.

fm.Color: Pale with darker lateral bands; keeled

hp: Garden and crop pest. Herbivore on cultivated plants. Preys on slug. Family Limacidae.

desc: Rock-dwelling shoulderband — native snail of rocky hillsides. Found under rocks and in crevices. Restricted to specific geological formations.
hp: Native CA land snail — calcium-dependent. Chaparral/oak woodland leaf litter. Preys on snail. Family Helminthoglyptidae.

desc: Quick gloss snail — tiny translucent amber snail of leaf litter. Fast-moving for a snail. Very common in moist gardens under debris. Important decomposer.

fm.Color: Translucent amber; fast-moving for a snail

hp: Detritivore in leaf litter — nutrient cycling. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Circumscribed murex — small ornate marine snail of rocky intertidal. Sculptured shell with spiral cords and axial ribs. Predatory on barnacles.
hp: Rocky intertidal predator — drills into mussels, barnacles, or other molluscs. Preys on snail. Family Muricidae. Circled.

desc: Tongued slipper shell — small marine snail attached inside empty shells and on rocks. Filter-feeder. Common in intertidal zone.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Family Calyptraeidae.

desc: Acute bladder snail — common freshwater snail with pointed sinistral (left-coiled) shell. Found in ponds, streams, even drainage ditches. Tolerates polluted water.
hp: Stream and river habitats. Aquatic or semi-aquatic lifestyle. Preys on snail. Family Physidae.

desc: Thick-shelled venus — marine bivalve of sandy subtidal habitats. Heavy thick shell. Found washed up on beaches. Native.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. On Fraxinus (ash). Beach habitat.

desc: California coffee bean trivia — tiny marine snail with polished coffee-bean-shaped shell. Found under rocks in low intertidal zone. One of two trivia species in LA tidepools.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Tiny. Coffee.

desc: Spectacular corolla — pelagic sea butterfly (pteropod). Tiny transparent marine snail that 'flies' through open water using wing-like parapodia. Washes ashore occasionally.
hp: Marine invertebrate of socal waters. On Fraxinus (ash). Tiny. Spectacular.

desc: Asian apple snail — large freshwater snail with thick spiral shell. Invasive from China. Found in aquarium trade and occasionally in warm LA waterways. Quarantine concern.
hp: Pome fruit associate — on apple or pear (Malus/Pyrus).

desc: Small pointed snail — tiny elongated snail with pointed shell. Introduced from Mediterranean. Very common in coastal disturbed habitats. Aggregates on posts and fences.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail.

desc: Brown-streaked sapsucker — tiny sea slug that sucks sap from green algae. Bright green from ingested chloroplasts that continue photosynthesizing inside the slug.
hp: Marine algae grazer — important in controlling algal growth on rocks. Preys on slug. Tiny. Brown-streaked.

desc: Hedgpeth's sapsucker — tiny sea slug that feeds on green algae and retains functional chloroplasts ('solar-powered slug'). Can photosynthesize for weeks without eating.
hp: Marine algae grazer — important in controlling algal growth on rocks. Preys on slug. Tiny. Hedgpeth's.

desc: Scale-sided piddock — boring marine bivalve that drills into rock using rasping shell ridges. Creates characteristic round holes in intertidal sandstone. Scale-like projections diagnostic.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Family Pholadidae.

desc: Red-rimmed melania — freshwater snail with elongated shell and red-brown color banding. Invasive from Asia/Africa. Found in warm springs and aquarium trade escapees.
hp: Red-rimmed melania — invasive freshwater snail from Africa/Asia. Intermediate host for trematode parasites. Parthenogenetic — rapid colonizer.

desc: Lance's doto — tiny marine nudibranch with club-shaped cerata. Found on hydroids in intertidal and subtidal zones. One of several Doto species in LA waters.
hp: Intertidal nudibranch — specialist predator of sessile marine invertebrates. Tiny. Lance's.

desc: California frog shell — marine snail with ornate sculptured shell. Predatory — feeds on marine worms. Found in rocky subtidal. Beautiful shell collected by beachcombers.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Beach habitat.

desc: Channeled basket snail — small marine snail with channeled suture. Scavenger on mudflats and in shallow subtidal. Common in sandy-mud habitats.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Preys on snail. Scavenger.

desc: Seal doto — tiny marine nudibranch. Club-shaped cerata with dark pigment rings. On hydroids in rocky intertidal. Named from Greek for 'blue'.
hp: Intertidal nudibranch — specialist predator of sessile marine invertebrates. Tiny. Seal.

desc: Lovely vallonia — tiny disc-shaped snail (2-3mm) in leaf litter and under rocks. Widespread. One of the most common but overlooked micro-snails in gardens.
hp: Smooth grass snail — tiny (2mm) European land snail in garden leaf litter and grassland. Detritivore. Calcium-dependent thin shell.

desc: Chestnut slug — small invasive slug spreading rapidly through LA. Pale brown with chestnut markings. Garden pest. Recently separated from D. panormitanum.
hp: Garden and crop pest. Herbivore on cultivated plants. Preys on slug. Family Agriolimacidae.

desc: Pacific ancula — small white marine nudibranch with orange-tipped cerata. Found on bryozoans in subtidal zone. Delicate and beautiful.
hp: Marine nudibranch on invertebrate colonies. Family Proctonotidae.

desc: Appleseed erato — tiny polished marine snail resembling an apple seed. Tan-pink with three dark dorsal grooves. Under rocks in low intertidal. Related to cowries.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. On apple.

desc: Painted spindle — elongated marine snail with yellow-brown banded shell. Predatory — drills into other molluscs. Rocky subtidal habitat.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Elongated. Painted.

desc: Western mud nassa — small marine snail scavenging on mudflats. Elongated shell with crown-like sculpture at suture. Common in sheltered bays.
hp: Marine invertebrate of socal waters. Preys on snail. Scavenger.

desc: Strong's sidegill slug — marine slug with lateral gill plume. Found in kelp beds and rocky subtidal. Named for malacologist A.M. Strong.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat on rocky substrate. Preys on slug. Family Pleurobranchidae.

desc: Marsh ramshorn — freshwater snail with flat disc-shaped shell. Common in ponds and marshes. Important intermediate host for trematode parasites.
hp: Ram's horn snail — freshwater. Intermediate host for trematode parasites including swimmer's itch cercariae. Water quality indicator.

desc: Hemphill's westernslug — native slug of moist foothill canyons. California endemic. Under logs and leaf litter in coast live oak woodland. Declining due to habitat loss.
hp: On coast live oak (quercus agrifolia). Associated with Coast Live Oak. On Quercus (oak). Woodland. Hemphill's.

desc: Slotted lancetooth snail — native predatory snail that eats other snails. Shell with distinctive slot-like umbilicus. Important native species of foothill canyons.
hp: Snail/slug predator. Foothill habitat.

desc: Norris's slipper shell — small marine snail living stacked on turban snails. Filter-feeder. Sequential hermaphrodite — starts male, becomes female with age.

fm.Color: Brown; flat slipper shape; on turban snails

hp: Norris's slipper limpet — marine filter feeder on kelp holdfasts and rocky substrate. Protandrous hermaphrodite — born male, becomes female.

desc: Enteromorpha sapsucker — tiny sacoglossan sea slug feeding on green algae. Retains functional chloroplasts from algae — can photosynthesize ('solar-powered slug').
hp: Marine algae grazer — important in controlling algal growth on rocks. Preys on slug. Tiny. Enteromorpha-eating.

desc: Pacific corambe — tiny marine nudibranch living on and feeding on bryozoans. Well-camouflaged — matches host bryozoan colony color. Easily overlooked.
hp: Cryptically camouflaged against habitat substrate. Tiny. Well-camouflaged — matches host bryozoan colony color.

desc: Santa Catalina Island snail — red-banded native land snail of Santa Catalina Island. Channel Islands endemic. Part of the remarkable island snail diversity.
hp: Native land snail of coastal/foothill habitats. Preys on snail. Family Helminthoglyptidae.

desc: Western strawberry cockle — marine bivalve with distinctively angled shell. Found in sandy subtidal habitats. Shell ribbed with two angular ridges.

fm.Color: White with brown marks; angular ribs

hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. On strawberr.

desc: Hunchback doris — white marine nudibranch with humped back covered in small tubercles. Found on sponges in rocky subtidal. Feeds on sponges.
hp: Marine nudibranch on invertebrate colonies. Family Discodorididae.

desc: Carpenter's carditid — small marine bivalve with strongly ribbed shell. Attached to rocks by byssus threads. Named for malacologist P.P. Carpenter.
hp: Rocky marine habitat — attached to or under rocks in subtidal zone. Family Carditidae.

desc: Pacific white hoof shell — small marine snail with cap-like shell. Attaches to rocks and other shells in intertidal zone. Filter-feeder.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Family Hipponicidae.

desc: Snakeskin turrid — small elongated marine snail with snakeskin-like pattern. Predatory — uses venomous radular tooth to paralyze prey. Rocky subtidal.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Elongated. Predatory — uses venomous radular tooth to paralyze.

desc: Ida's mitre — elongated marine snail with spindle-shaped shell. Predatory on worms and other invertebrates. Found in sandy subtidal habitats.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Elongated. Elongated marine snail with spindle-shaped shell.

desc: Keen's tritonid — medium marine nudibranch. Feeds on soft corals and sea pens. Branched cerata. Found in rocky subtidal habitats.
hp: Marine nudibranch on invertebrate colonies. Family Tritoniidae.

desc: Dall's dwarf turban — tiny marine snail with iridescent shell interior. Found on rocks in subtidal zone. Shell often encrusted with coralline algae.
hp: Dark homalopoma — tiny marine snail grazing microalgae on subtidal rocks. Part of diverse rocky reef gastropod community.

desc: Fat nassa — small plump marine scavenger snail on sandy mudflats. Detects food by chemoreception. Gathers in numbers at carrion on seafloor.
hp: On carrion — essential decomposer. Preys on snail.

desc: Avalon Island snail — small native land snail endemic to Catalina Island (Avalon area). Part of the unique Channel Islands endemic snail fauna. Conservation interest.
hp: Native land snail of coastal/foothill habitats. Conservation concern. Preys on snail. Family Helminthoglyptidae. On Island snail.

desc: Pearly jingle — thin iridescent marine bivalve cemented to rocks and shells. Lower valve has hole for byssus. Makes jingling sound when shells wash up on beach.
hp: Rocky marine habitat — attached to or under rocks in subtidal zone. On Fraxinus (ash).

desc: Urak doto — tiny marine nudibranch with distinctive club-shaped cerata. On hydroids in rocky habitats. One of several Doto species in SoCal waters.
hp: Marine nudibranch on invertebrate colonies. Tiny.

desc: Pacific eggcockle — smooth oval marine bivalve found in sandy subtidal. Thin fragile shell with fine radial ribs. Occasionally washes ashore.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. On Fraxinus (ash). Family Cardiidae.

desc: Soledad desertsnail — native California land snail of foothill canyons. Restricted range. Found under rocks in moist microhabitats. Conservation concern.
hp: Desert and desert-edge habitats. Preys on snail.

desc: Greater argonaut — pelagic octopus. Female secretes a beautiful paper-thin spiral shell ('paper nautilus') for egg brooding. Shells occasionally wash ashore. Not a true nautilus.

fm.Note: Female-only shell for egg brooding — not a true nautilus

hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. On Fraxinus (ash).

desc: Elongated violet snail — pelagic marine snail that floats on a raft of air bubbles. Purple shell. Washes ashore after storms. Feeds on by-the-wind sailors (Velella).
hp: Marine invertebrate of socal waters. On Fraxinus (ash). Elongated.

desc: Carpenter's turrid — elongated marine snail with turreted shell. Predatory — uses venomous radular tooth. Deep subtidal on soft bottoms.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Elongated. Elongated marine snail with turreted shell.

desc: Minute gem snail — tiny translucent snail (2-3mm) in leaf litter worldwide. One of the most widespread land snails. Nearly invisible without magnification.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny. Minute.

desc: Dark unicorn — dark marine snail with spine-like process near aperture. Predatory on barnacles and mussels. Rocky intertidal. Drills through prey shells.
hp: Corn (Zea mays) associate — larvae feed on roots, silk, or kernels. On Pinus (pine).

desc: Ribbed hoof snail — small cap-shaped marine snail cemented to rocks and other shells. Strongly ribbed. Common in intertidal zone.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Family Hipponicidae.

desc: Ford's venus — marine bivalve with concentric ribbing. Sandy and muddy subtidal habitats. Named for Ford. Part of diverse venus clam family.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Family Veneridae.

desc: Spiny cup-and-saucer — distinctive marine snail with internal shelf ('saucer') inside cup-shaped shell. Spiny exterior. On rocks in intertidal.

fm.Color: Brown; cup-shaped with internal shelf; spiny

hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Spiny/armed.

desc: Brushed turrid — elongated marine snail with brush-like shell sculpture. Predatory — uses venomous radula. Sandy subtidal habitat.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Elongated. Brushed.

desc: Dim rocksnail — small marine snail of rocky intertidal. Predatory on barnacles. Foveolate (pitted) shell sculpture distinctive.

fm.Color: Brown; pitted shell; on intertidal rocks

hp: Rocky intertidal predator — drills into mussels, barnacles, or other molluscs. Preys on snail. Family Muricidae. Dim.

desc: Steinberg's corambe — tiny marine nudibranch living on bryozoans. Nearly invisible — matches host colony. Named for biologist Steinberg.
hp: Marine nudibranch on invertebrate colonies. Tiny.

desc: Wart-necked piddock — small boring bivalve drilling into soft rock in intertidal zone. Shell with rasping ridges for boring. Distinctive wart-like siphon.

fm.Color: White; boring into rock; warty siphon

hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Family Pholadidae.

desc: Oval ambersnail — small translucent amber snail of moist habitats near water. Very thin fragile shell. Found on vegetation near streams and ponds.
hp: Stream and river habitats. Aquatic or semi-aquatic lifestyle. Preys on snail. Family Succineidae.

desc: Soledad shoulderband snail — native shoulderband snail restricted to specific spring-fed habitats. Part of the Helminthoglypta radiation — a California-endemic snail genus.
hp: On elm (Ulmus) — urban tree defoliator.

desc: Sullied pheasant shell — tiny marine snail with iridescent shell. On rocks and algae in intertidal and subtidal zones. Beautiful coloring under magnification.
hp: Rocky intertidal algae grazer on LA County shores. Important in structuring algal community. Preys on snail. Iridescent.

desc: Red aldisa — small red marine nudibranch. Feeds on sponges in rocky subtidal zone. Distinctive blood-red coloring.
hp: Marine nudibranch on invertebrate colonies. Family Cadlinidae.

desc: Graceful rocksnail — small ornate marine snail of rocky intertidal. Sculptured shell with spiral cords. Predatory — drills into barnacles and mussels.
hp: Rocky intertidal predator — drills into mussels, barnacles, or other molluscs. Preys on snail. Family Muricidae. Graceful.

desc: Green garden snail — medium garden snail with distinctive green periostracum when fresh. Introduced from Mediterranean. In gardens. Less common than brown garden snail.
hp: Garden snail/slug. Nocturnal herbivore. Preys on snail. Family Helicidae.

desc: Common chrysalis-snail — tiny cylindrical land snail (3mm). Introduced from Europe. In moist leaf litter and under rocks. Widespread but requires magnification to see.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny. Common.

desc: Nuttall lucine — small round marine bivalve of sandy mudflats. White shell with fine concentric ribs. Harbors symbiotic sulfur-oxidizing bacteria in gills.
hp: Sandy subtidal marine habitat — burrows in or on sandy bottom.

desc: Monterey cerithiopsid — tiny elongated marine snail with spiral cords. On sponges in subtidal zone. Part of the diverse micro-gastropod fauna.
hp: Subtidal marine invertebrate of SoCal waters. Preys on snail. Elongated.

desc: Button shell — tiny limpet-like marine snail of high intertidal crevices. Round flat shell with reticulate sculpture. Filter-feeder — unusual for a snail.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Flattened body.

desc: Vasquez shoulderband — native California land snail with distinctive shoulderband shell pattern. Very restricted range. NatureServe conservation concern. Part of the endemic radiation of Helminthoglypta shoulderband snails — LA County's most diverse native snail genus.
hp: Native land snail of coastal/foothill habitats. Conservation concern. Preys on snail. Family Helminthoglyptidae. Vasquez.

desc: Birdlike chiton — small chiton with raised butterfly/bird-shaped valve sculpture. Rocky intertidal. One of the more ornate chitons.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Family Acanthochitonidae.

desc: One-groove pedipes — tiny air-breathing marine snail of splash zone. Between truly marine and terrestrial. In spray zone rock crevices.
hp: Marine invertebrate of socal waters. On Fraxinus (ash). Tiny. One-groove.

desc: California softshell — tiny burrowing marine bivalve living commensally in burrows of other organisms (ghost shrimp, innkeeper worm). Nestles in host burrow wall.
hp: Marine filter feeder — processes seawater for food particles.

desc: Bodega tellin — thin flat marine bivalve of sandy habitat. Deposit-feeds with long siphon. Common in sandy subtidal.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Flattened body.

desc: Chace's sidegill slug — marine slug with lateral gill. Subtidal on rocky reefs. Part of the pleurobranchid slug family.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat on rocky substrate. Preys on slug. Family Pleurobranchidae.

desc: Mimic lymnaea — freshwater snail with elongated translucent shell. In ponds and ditches. Can serve as intermediate host for liver fluke parasites.
hp: Mimic lymnaea — freshwater snail. Intermediate host for liver fluke (Fasciola hepatica). Veterinary importance for livestock grazing near water.

desc: Blind snail — tiny blind subterranean snail living entirely underground. Shell transparent and needle-shaped. Found when digging in gardens. Never seen on surface.
hp: Garden snail/slug. Nocturnal herbivore. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Navisa dove snail — tiny marine snail (olivella) of sandy subtidal habitat. Polished, glossy shell. Burrows just beneath sand surface. Shell used extensively by indigenous Chumash and Tongva peoples for shell bead money ('olivella money beads'). Still common offshore.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. On Fraxinus (ash). Tiny.

desc: California vertigo — extremely tiny land snail (2mm). In moist leaf litter. One of the smallest snails in LA County. Requires a hand lens to see.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny. California.

desc: Indented shoulderband — native California land snail with diagnostic indentation on shell body whorl. Part of the endemic Helminthoglypta radiation. Conservation concern — habitat loss from development. Chaparral and oak woodland in LA County foothills.
hp: On coast live oak (quercus agrifolia). Associated with Coast Live Oak. On Quercus (oak). Chaparral. Indented.

desc: Red thordisa — reddish marine nudibranch. On sponges in subtidal rocky reefs. Part of the dorid nudibranch family.
hp: Marine nudibranch on invertebrate colonies. Family Discodorididae.

desc: Ash gyro — tiny flat-coiled freshwater ramshorn snail. In ponds, ditches, and slow streams. Feeds on algae and detritus. Shell diameter only 4-6mm.
hp: Stream and river habitats. Aquatic or semi-aquatic lifestyle. On Fraxinus (ash).

desc: Middle Canyon spring pyrg — tiny freshwater snail endemic to specific springs in LA County. Restricted to spring habitats. Critical conservation concern.
hp: Freshwater snail in LA County streams and ponds. Water quality indicator.

desc: California truncatella — tiny marine snail of splash zone and wrack. Truncated shell — apex broken off in adults. In debris at high tide line.
hp: Marine invertebrate of socal waters. On Fraxinus (ash). Tiny. California.

desc: Red-lined pheasant shell — tiny colorful marine snail with red spiral lines. On algae-covered rocks in low intertidal.

fm.Color: Pale with red spiral lines; on algae

hp: Rocky intertidal algae grazer on LA County shores. Important in structuring algal community. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: San Clemente Island blunt-top snail — extremely tiny land snail. Named from San Clemente Island. In leaf litter. Requires magnification.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny. San.

desc: Penciled dove snail — small marine snail with fine axial ribbing ('penciled'). Rocky intertidal. On algae and under rocks.
hp: Rocky intertidal algae grazer on LA County shores. Important in structuring algal community. Preys on snail. Family Columbellidae.

desc: San Pedro auger — elongated marine snail with drill-like spiral shell. Named for San Pedro, CA. Sandy subtidal. Predatory.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Elongated. San.

desc: Helga's turbonilla — tiny (3mm) marine ectoparasitic snail. Feeds on body fluids of other marine invertebrates — tubeworms, bivalves, and other sessile animals. Uses modified radula to pierce host. Part of the species-rich Pyramidellidae — all are parasites of other marine life.
hp: Helga's pyramidellid — tiny (3mm) marine ectoparasite piercing tubeworms and bivalves with stylet. Part of diverse Pyramidellidae.

desc: Striped barrel snail — tiny marine snail with barrel-shaped shell and incised spiral grooves. Sandy subtidal habitat. Uncommon.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Preys on snail. Tiny. Striped.

desc: Engel's gumdrop — small orange marine slug. Rounded gumdrop shape. On sponges in subtidal zone. One of the more colorful slugs.
hp: Subtidal marine invertebrate of SoCal waters. Preys on slug. Family Pleurobranchidae.

desc: Rough barrel-bubble — tiny marine snail with barrel-shaped shell and rough surface texture. Sandy subtidal habitat. Part of the Acteonidae — ancient gastropods retaining an operculum and internal shell features considered primitive. Burrows in soft sediment.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Preys on snail. Tiny. Rough.

desc: Green falsejingle — marine bivalve attached to rocks. Green iridescent interior. Thin fragile shell. In rocky subtidal.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat on rocky substrate.

desc: Catalina mountainsnail — large native land snail endemic to Santa Catalina Island. Found in rocky canyons. One of the largest native land snails in the region.
hp: Native land snail of coastal/foothill habitats. Preys on snail. Montane.

desc: Flat-tip piddock — boring marine bivalve drilling into rock. Creates characteristic round holes in sandstone and mudstone. Flat shell tips used as rasps.
hp: On dock or sorrel (Rumex) — Polygonaceae.

desc: Black-footed tightcoil — tiny land snail with tightly coiled shell and dark foot. In moist leaf litter. Native.
hp: Native California land snail — endemic radiation in LA County foothills. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Costate vallonia — extremely tiny ribbed land snail (~2mm). Fine radial ribs on shell. In leaf litter under rocks. Widespread but microscopic.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny. Costate.

desc: Convex metaxia — tiny marine snail with spiral cords and axial ribs creating lattice pattern. Subtidal. Beautiful under magnification.
hp: Subtidal marine invertebrate of SoCal waters. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Cooper's turret — elongated spiral marine snail resembling a tiny tower. Sandy subtidal. Named for J.G. Cooper. Classic turritellid shape.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Preys on snail. Elongated.

desc: Transparent razor clam — elongated marine bivalve with thin translucent shell. In sandy subtidal. Fast burrower.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Elongated.

desc: Harp barrel-bubble — tiny marine snail with barrel-shaped shell and fine spiral ribs. Sandy subtidal. Part of diverse micro-gastropod fauna.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Preys on snail. Tiny. Hooked.

desc: Pigeon erato — tiny polished marine snail related to cowries. In low intertidal under rocks. Coffee-bean shaped.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Tiny. Pigeon.

desc: Swift trapania — tiny (10mm) marine nudibranch. Unusually fast-moving for a sea slug. On bryozoans in rocky subtidal. White with distinctive lateral processes. Part of the small family Goniodorididae. The name 'velox' (swift) reflects its active locomotion.
hp: Marine nudibranch on invertebrate colonies. Preys on slug.

desc: Nuttall's cockle — large edible marine bivalve with strong radial ribs. In sandy-mud bays. Historically important food source. Gapes characteristically.

fm.Color: White with brown; strong radial ribs

hp: Sandy subtidal marine habitat — burrows in or on sandy bottom. Preys on ant.

desc: Seminole ramshorn — medium freshwater snail with flat disc-shaped shell. In ponds and streams. Important intermediate host of trematode parasites.
hp: Seminole ram's horn — freshwater snail. Intermediate host for trematode parasites. Aquarium trade introduction now widespread in warm waters.

desc: Tawny glass-snail — tiny translucent amber land snail in leaf litter. Glass-like shell. Very common but easily overlooked. Important decomposer.
hp: Detritivore in leaf litter — nutrient cycling. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Santa Catalina lancetooth — predatory land snail endemic to Santa Catalina Island. Eats other snails. Conservation concern — very restricted range.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Family Haplotrematidae.

desc: American keeled slug — small native slug with dorsal keel. In moist foothill canyons. Under logs and leaf litter. Native California species.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on slug. Family Arionidae.

desc: Herald snail — extremely tiny land snail (2mm) of wet habitats. One of the smallest land snails. In moss and wet leaf litter. Requires hand lens.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Guadalupe lancetooth — predatory native land snail. Eats other snails and slugs. Named for Guadalupe Island. Restricted range.
hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Family Haplotrematidae.

desc: Mouse-eared snail — tiny salt marsh snail with ear-like shell shape. In upper marsh and splash zone. Air-breathing marine snail.
hp: Marine invertebrate of socal waters. On Fraxinus (ash). Marsh/wetland.

desc: Northwest striate — tiny land snail with fine striated shell. In moist leaf litter under native vegetation. Native.
hp: Native California land snail — endemic radiation in LA County foothills. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Black gloss snail — tiny shiny dark land snail in wet habitats. Glossy black shell. Under debris near water. Important wetland indicator species.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny. Black.

desc: Polished cystiscid — tiny (4mm) glossy marine snail with beautifully polished, smooth shell. Sandy subtidal habitat in shallow warm water. Part of the Cystiscidae — a family of minute marine snails often overlooked due to their size but diverse in tropical-subtropical waters.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Preys on snail. Tiny. Polished.

desc: Western baby's ear — flat ear-shaped marine snail. Soft body much larger than shell. Predatory — engulfs and feeds on bivalves. Sandy subtidal.

fm.Color: Pale; flat ear-shaped shell; huge soft body

hp: Specialist predator of snails and slugs. Preys on snail. Flattened body.

desc: Noted pyramid snail — tiny parasitic marine snail feeding on other invertebrates. Part of the diverse pyramid snail family.
hp: Noted pyramidellid — tiny marine ectoparasite feeding on body fluids of tubeworms and oysters. Modified radula pierces host tissue.

desc: Thin turbonilla — tiny very slender parasitic marine snail. Part of the diverse Turbonilla micro-gastropod genus.
hp: Slender turbonilla — tiny marine ectoparasite of sessile invertebrates. Extremely elongated shell. Part of diverse Pyramidellidae.

desc: Ridged teinostoma — extremely tiny marine snail with fine ridges. In sandy subtidal. Part of the tornid micro-gastropod family.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Preys on snail. Tiny. Ridged.

desc: Sharon's abalone — very small abalone-relative (not a true commercial abalone). On rocks in low intertidal. Ear-shaped shell.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Family Haliotidae.

desc: Semi-inflated snail — small native land snail of coastal sage scrub. Named for Henry Burch. California endemic. Conservation concern.
hp: In coastal sage scrub habitat. Associated with Black Sage. Preys on snail.

desc: California assiminea — tiny intertidal snail of salt marshes and estuaries. Amphibious — breathes air. At marsh-water interface.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Preys on snail. Marsh/wetland.

desc: Columbia doto — tiny marine nudibranch on hydroid colonies. Distinctive club-shaped cerata (back projections) with dark spots at tips. Rocky subtidal. Feeds exclusively on hydroids. Named for British Columbia (type locality). Cryptic — matching its hydroid substrate.
hp: Cryptically camouflaged against habitat substrate. Tiny. Feeds exclusively on hydroids.

desc: Stubby bobtail squid — small cephalopod burying in sandy bottom by day, hunting at night. Bioluminescent. Symbiotic bacteria in light organ. Fascinating biology.
hp: Bioluminescent — produces light for mate attraction or warning. Remarkable adaptation.

desc: Baily's miniature ark — tiny marine bivalve with ark-shaped ribbed shell. In rocky crevices. One of the smaller ark clams.
hp: Rocky marine habitat — attached to or under rocks in subtidal zone. Tiny.

desc: Giant egg cockle — very large smooth marine bivalve. Thin fragile shell. Sandy subtidal. Occasionally washes ashore. Impressive size.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. On Fraxinus (ash).

desc: Mountain whorl-snail — microscopic land snail (~2mm) of mountain habitats. In moss and leaf litter at higher elevations. Requires magnification.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Montane.

desc: Hard-margin keyhole limpet — small marine limpet with oval hole at shell apex. On rocks in low intertidal. Thickened shell margin.
hp: Rocky intertidal — tidepool habitat. Family Fissurellidae.

desc: Rimule-like slit shell — tiny marine snail with slit in shell margin. Primitive gastropod feature. Rocky subtidal.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat on rocky substrate. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Thin-shelled littleneck — marine bivalve with thin fragile shell. In sandy-mud subtidal. Related to commercial littleneck clams.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat in sandy bottom. Family Veneridae.

desc: Willowi sacoglossan — tiny sea slug feeding on algae. Retains functional chloroplasts from algal prey — partial photosynthesis ('solar-powered slug'). Estuarine.
hp: On willows (salix) in riparian habitat. On Salix (willow). Tiny.

desc: Japanese carinaria — pelagic heteropod — a sea snail that swims in open ocean. Transparent gelatinous body with tiny glass-like shell. Extraordinary open-ocean lifestyle.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny. Japanese.

desc: Two-rowed murex — medium predatory marine snail with two spiral rows of spines. Rocky intertidal. Drills into mussels and barnacles.

fm.Color: Brown; two rows of spines; predatory

hp: Rocky intertidal predator — drills into mussels and barnacles with radula. Two rows of shell spines. Important in intertidal food web.

desc: Windowed liotia — tiny marine snail with fenestrated (windowed/perforated) shell sculpture. Rocky subtidal. Beautiful under magnification.
hp: Subtidal marine habitat on rocky substrate. Preys on snail. Nest associate.

desc: Kelsey's auger snail — tiny (5mm) elongated parasitic marine snail. Ectoparasite — feeds on body fluids of tubeworms and other sessile invertebrates. Named for collector F.W. Kelsey. Part of the extremely species-rich Pyramidellidae, the largest family of parasitic gastropods.
hp: Kelsey's turbonilla — tiny marine ectoparasite. Feeds on body fluids of tubeworms and bivalves using modified piercing radula.

desc: San Pedro triphora — tiny marine snail with sinistral (left-coiling) shell — opposite of most snails. On sponges. Named for San Pedro, CA.
hp: Marine invertebrate of socal waters. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Pupoid phasianella — tiny marine snail with pupiform (pupa-shaped) shell. On algae in low intertidal. Part of diverse micro-gastropod fauna.
hp: Rocky intertidal algae grazer on LA County shores. Important in structuring algal community. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Steep-backed pyramid snail — tiny marine ectoparasitic snail. Feeds on body fluids of marine worms and bivalves.
hp: Steep-backed pyramidellid — tiny marine ectoparasite. Pierces hosts (tubeworms, bivalves) with stylet. LA County subtidal rocky habitat.

desc: Smooth-based auger snail — tiny elongated parasitic marine snail with characteristically smooth (non-ribbed) lower whorls. Ectoparasite of various marine invertebrates. Part of the massive Pyramidellidae radiation. Distinguished from other Turbonilla by the smooth basal whorls.
hp: Smooth-based turbonilla — marine ectoparasite with diagnostic smooth basal whorls. On various marine invertebrate hosts.

desc: California pedicularia — tiny parasitic marine snail living on gorgonian corals. Feeds on coral tissue. Part of the poorly known parasite snail guild.
hp: California pedicularia — marine ectoparasite living on gorgonian coral colonies. Feeds on coral tissue. Rocky subtidal specialist.

desc: Beautiful cerithiopsid — tiny marine snail on sponges in subtidal. Part of the diverse but overlooked micro-gastropod fauna.
hp: Subtidal marine invertebrate of SoCal waters. Preys on snail. Tiny.

desc: Cat vayssierea — tiny bright orange marine slug. On bryozoans in subtidal. Part of the poorly known Okadaiidae family.
hp: Subtidal marine invertebrate of SoCal waters. Preys on slug. Tiny.

desc: Beautiful vitrinid — tiny semi-slug with glassy shell partially covering body. In moist habitats. Shell too small for full retraction. Intermediate between snail and slug.
hp: Soil/litter invertebrate — calcium-dependent shell. Part of detritivore community. Preys on snail. Tiny. Beautiful.
